title: Modernize Legacy Software with AI Agents
description: Build a portal that replaces legacy call tasks using Cursor, Supabase, and Retell AI for automated customer outreach.

cards.title: Replace Legacy Tools with AI
cards.body: You'll build a portal that takes a phone number and task description, automatically calls customers using AI, and tracks results. This replaces manual calling workflows with automated AI agents.

cards.title: Architecture Overview
cards.body: We're building a three-part system that works together seamlessly.
bullets: Portal: Web interface for submitting calls (Cursor + Claude)
Database: Shared storage for call data (Supabase)
AI Caller: Voice agent that makes actual calls (Retell AI)
Automation: Workflow that connects everything (n8n)

analogy.text: Think of it like a modern call center where the portal is your desk, Supabase is the filing cabinet, and Retell AI is your AI assistant making the calls.

cards.title: Clone the Starter Template
cards.body: Start with Bart's pre-built template that includes authentication, dashboard, and Supabase integration.
cards.code:
git clone https://github.com/your-repo/starter-template.git modernize-portal
cd modernize-portal
npm install
cards.codeCaption: This gives you login, dashboard, and logout pages already wired up

source.author: Bart Slodyczka
source.description: Cloning the starter template and installing dependencies

cards.title: Configure Environment Variables
cards.body: Set up your Supabase connection by creating the environment file and adding your credentials.
helpItems: Go to Supabase → Project Settings → Data API
Copy your Project URL and paste into SUPABASE_URL
Copy your Publishable Key and paste into SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY

cards.title: Create Test User in Supabase
cards.body: Before you can log into your portal, create a user account in Supabase Authentication.
bullets: Go to Supabase → Authentication → Users
Click 'Create a new user'
Use any test email and strong password
Check 'Automatically confirm user'
cards.safe: This is just for testing - you're creating a secure user account in your own database

cards.title: Test the Portal Locally
cards.body: Run the development server and verify everything works.
cards.codeCaption: Open the localhost URL and log in with your test user

source.author: Bart Slodyczka
source.description: Testing the login and viewing the basic dashboard

cards.title: Quick Check
cards.question: What role does Supabase play in this architecture?

options.text: It makes the AI phone calls
It stores call data and provides authentication

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Supabase is your shared database and handles user authentication.
cards.wrongFeedback: Supabase is the database layer - it stores your call records and handles user login.

cards.title: Planning the New Call Page
cards.body: Before building, let's think about what data we need to collect and store.
bullets: Input fields: Phone number and task description
Submit action: Save to Supabase and trigger AI call
Database schema: Store number, task, call status, transcript, recording

analogy.text: Like a digital version of writing call instructions on a sticky note, but it automatically triggers the call and tracks results.

cards.title: Ask Claude Code to Build the Page
cards.body: Use Claude Code in Cursor to generate the 'New Call' page with proper Supabase integration.
cards.code:
I want to create a new page called 'New Call' with:
- Phone number input field
- Short description field for the task
- Submit button that saves to Supabase
- Table schema that includes: phone, task, status, transcript, recording

Focus on the portal and Supabase side for now.
cards.codeCaption: Claude will generate the React components and Supabase table schema

source.author: Bart Slodyczka
source.description: Using Claude Code to generate the new call page and database schema

cards.title: Portal Foundation Complete!
cards.body: You've successfully set up the web portal with authentication, database connection, and a form to submit calls. Next, we'll build the AI voice agent.

cards.title: Create Retell AI Voice Agent
cards.body: Set up a simple voice agent that can handle appointment confirmations.
bullets: Go to Retell AI dashboard
Create new Voice Agent → Single Prompt Agent
Keep the prompt simple for testing
cards.code:
You are calling to confirm an appointment. Be polite and brief:

1. Ask to speak with the person
2. Confirm the appointment: {{appointment_details}}
3. Thank them and end the call

Use {{appointment_details}} for dynamic information.
cards.warn: Start simple! You can always make the agent more sophisticated after testing

cards.title: Connecting Everything with n8n
cards.body: n8n acts as the glue between your portal, AI agent, and database updates.
bullets: Trigger: New submission from your portal
Action 1: Fire outbound call via Retell AI
Action 2: Update Supabase row with call results

analogy.text: Think of n8n like a spider sitting in the web - when something happens in one part (form submission), it automatically triggers actions in other parts (call + database update).

cards.title: Build the n8n Workflow
cards.body: Create the automation that connects form submission to AI calling and result tracking.
bullets: Set up webhook trigger for form submissions
Add Retell AI node to initiate outbound calls
Add Supabase node to update call status
Test with a real phone number
helpItems: Use webhook URL from n8n in your portal's submit function
Pass phone number and task description to Retell AI
Update the database row with call completion status

source.author: Bart Slodyczka
source.description: Setting up the n8n workflow and testing the complete system

cards.title: System Check
cards.question: What happens when you click 'Submit Call' in the portal?

options.text: Saves to database, triggers n8n workflow, initiates AI call, updates status

cards.correctFeedback: Perfect! The submit triggers a complete workflow chain reaction.
cards.wrongFeedback: The submit button kicks off the entire workflow - database save, n8n trigger, AI call, and status update.

cards.title: Test the Complete System
cards.body: Time for the moment of truth - test your AI-powered call system end-to-end.
bullets: Enter your own phone number in the portal
Add a simple appointment confirmation task
Click submit and wait for your phone to ring
Answer the call and interact with your AI agent
Check the portal to see updated call status
cards.safe: Testing with your own number first ensures you can control the experience
troubleshoot: If call doesn't come through, check n8n webhook logs
If agent sounds robotic, simplify the Retell AI prompt
If status doesn't update, verify Supabase webhook connection

cards.title: Scaling Your AI System
cards.body: Now that you have the foundation, here's how to expand and improve.
bullets: Add complexity: Handle objections, reschedule requests, multiple scenarios
Improve data: Store call recordings, sentiment analysis, success rates
Scale operations: Bulk upload contacts, scheduled calling, team management
Enhance UX: Real-time call status, better reporting, mobile optimization

cards.title: Legacy System Modernized!
cards.body: You've successfully replaced a manual calling process with an AI-powered system. Your portal can now automatically handle customer outreach, track results, and scale infinitely. This same pattern can modernize any legacy workflow.
